title: pasta: timerfd/socket fd leak causing 100% CPU on single core / Unifi OS Server
body:
### Environment

- **Host**: Debian 13 VM on Proxmox
- **App**: UniFi OS Server (UOS Server 5.0.6) 
- **pasta version**: `0.0~git20250503.587980c-2+deb13u1`
---

### Issue Description

Every days of uptime, one CPU core gets pinned at 100%. The culprit is the `pasta` process used as the rootless network backend for the container.

**Root cause identified**: massive file descriptor leak. After ~10 minutes from boot the fd count is already ~3000 and keeps growing. After a few days it reaches ~262,000 fd.

Breakdown of leaked fd types:
- ~131,067 `anon_inode:[timerfd]`
- ~131,153 `socket`
- ~74 `pipe`

**Confirmed via**:
```
cat /proc//wchan  → 0   (not sleeping, busy-looping in userspace)
cat /proc//stack  → empty (no kernel syscall)
ls /proc//fd | wc -l  → 262,301 after a few days
```

Restarting the container clears the fd count and brings CPU back to normal immediately.

---

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install Debian 13 VM (Proxmox)
2. Install UOS Server module (deploys UniFi OS as a rootless Podman container)
3. Leave it running for a few hours/days until the UOS Server webui stops responding
4. Observe one CPU core at 100%, `pasta` fd count growing unboundedly

---

### Expected behavior

`pasta` fd count should stay stable over time. No CPU saturation.

---

### Workaround

Periodically restart the container (e.g. via cron). This resets `pasta` and clears all leaked fd.

---

comments.body:
@denispollini we only support the latest upstream versions here.

Can you please try with an updated version (for example, building it from source or using the package from Debian testing/unstable)?

This issue is most likely fixed by [these commits](https://passt.top/passt/log/?qt=range&q=eb3babf7b4cf3793e0ed48030db958ace6546d7e..d2f7c21cfb949f2b1587b9475917efdd6ac549fd).

If you can confirm that, and this is critical for you, it would be helpful to mention that in a Debian ticket.

Confirmed. The bundled pasta binary used by UOS Server is version 
2025_09_19.623dbf6, which predates the fix range (2026-02-25).

The system /usr/bin/pasta is 0.0~git20250503 (also older than the fix).

So the fix is not present in either binary. Will try replacing the 
bundled binary with a version built from a commit after d2f7c21c and 
report back.

Solution:

We compiled pasta from the latest source and replaced the bundled binary shipped with UOS Server.
Steps:

Install build dependencies:

`sudo apt install -y git make gcc libcap-dev libseccomp-dev`

Clone the passt repository:

```
git clone https://passt.top/passt
cd passt
```

Compile pasta:

`make pasta`
This produces version 2026_01_20.386b5f5-90-gec96f01 (90 commits after the fix range).

Stop the container, replace the bundled binary and restart:

```
uosserver stop
sudo cp /var/lib/uosserver/bin/pasta /var/lib/uosserver/bin/pasta.bak
sudo cp ./pasta /var/lib/uosserver/bin/pasta
uosserver start
```

Verify the new version is in use:

`/var/lib/uosserver/bin/pasta --version`
pasta 2026_01_20.386b5f5-90-gec96f01

> We compiled pasta from the latest source and replaced the bundled binary shipped with UOS Server.

Thanks for the follow-up. I'm glad the latest version fixes the issue.

By the way, I shipped updated packages for Debian (at the moment just unstable) meanwhile, that's version 0.0~git20260507.1afd4ed-1 (https://tracker.debian.org/news/1749728/accepted-passt-00git202605071afd4ed-1-source-into-unstable/).

Feel free to close this if you don't see the reported issue anymore.

**Update after 22 days of uptime:**
The issue reappeared after ~22 days (previously it occurred within a few days), so the fix partially mitigated the leak but did not resolve it completely.
fd count reached 262,301 again, with the same breakdown (timerfd + socket leak).
The compiled binary 2026_01_20.386b5f5-90-gec96f01 is still in use:
`/var/lib/uosserver/bin/pasta --version`
pasta 2026_01_20.386b5f5-90-gec96f01
The fix extended the time before the leak occurs from a few days to ~22 days, but the root cause is still present. Any further suggestions?


Update: UOS Server auto-update overwrites the patched binary
UOS Server automatically updated to 5.0.8 (via its built-in updater service uosserver-updater.service), overwriting our manually compiled pasta binary with the old bundled version:
/var/lib/uosserver/bin/pasta --version
pasta 2025_09_19.623dbf6
This means every time UOS Server updates, the fix needs to be reapplied manually. We have replaced the binary again with our compiled version (2026_01_20.386b5f5-90-gec96f01).
It would be very helpful if Ubiquiti could update the bundled pasta binary to a version that includes the fix, or if there is a way to prevent the binary from being overwritten on updates.

> The fix extended the time before the leak occurs from a few days to ~22 days, but the root cause is still present. Any further suggestions?

Thanks for following up. The upstream repository now contains an [additional fix](https://passt.top/passt/commit/?id=4b2823784aab04a70dfc295b16fd6f0592955790) that might be related to the issue you are seeing. Could you try that out?

> We have replaced the binary again with our compiled version (2026_01_20.386b5f5-90-gec96f01). It would be very helpful if Ubiquiti could update the bundled pasta binary to a version that includes the fix, or if there is a way to prevent the binary from being overwritten on updates.

I think you need to ask Ubiquiti for that.
